title: My Niece Disowned Me After I Co-Signed Her Bakery Loan. So I Watched Her Lose It.
description: I co-signed a $190,000 loan for my niece’s artisanal bakery, trusting the 40% stake she promised in writing. On opening day she handed me a zero-equity silent-investor contract and kicked me out. I discovered she falsified revenue projections to the bank, reported her, and stood by as the bank recalled the loan, then bought her equipment and lease at the distress sale for just $11,000. Now her handwritten promise note hangs on the Wall of Broken Promises in my new pie shop. #RevengeStory #FamilyBetrayal #InstantKarma
thumb_text: I FRAMED HER BROKEN PROMISE IN MY SHOP
pinned_comment: If your niece handed you a zero-equity contract after you co-signed her $190,000 bakery loan, what would you have done? Tell me your story in the comments.

thumb_lines.text: I CO-SIGNED HER $190,000 ARTISANAL BAKERY LOAN
HER FRAUD GOT THE LOAN RECALLED
